demographic_audit_note: Audited Atlantis demographics against SNF face sheet; found that the patient's primary phone was listed as the facility's main line. Corrected it to her personal cell and verified Aetna Medicare Advantage is active and in-network for preferred HHA providers.
home_vs_ltc_determination: Social Worker (SW) confirmed the goal is Home with HHA and PT support, as the patient has a strong desire to return to her apartment despite post-chemo fatigue.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: Patient is meeting PT goals for stair navigation, but the daughter (POA) is expressing extreme anxiety about managing the patient's nausea and weakness at home, calling the SW to request a 7-day discharge delay.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card inserted into patient's iPhone contacts; physical program flyer delivered to daughter during the weekly care conference to reinforce the 24-hour support model.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Conducted 24-hour pulse call. Reached the daughter who was in a state of 'Caregiver_Panic', threatening to refuse the discharge transport. De-escalated by confirming the MA visit window and reviewing the physical appointment card details.
atlantis_final_sync: Entered Discharge Date (11/14) and 1st Visit Date (11/15) into the Atlantis transition module.
ma_visit_booking: Medical Assistant (MA) successfully scheduled for the first home visit on 11/15 at 10:00 AM, strictly within the 24-hour post-discharge window.
narrative_summary: Initiated Triage for a 74yo post-chemo patient, correcting critical contact data in Atlantis during the initial audit. Managed significant 'Caregiver_Panic' during the Maintenance stage by providing the daughter with concrete expectations and a physical appointment card. Secured a successful transition home by scheduling a 24-hour MA follow-up, overcoming family-driven friction to trigger the first visit incentive.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Caregiver_Panic: Daughter expresses fear of managing post-chemo side effects alone.
format_1_state_log.ai_assumed_bottleneck: Family readiness and support system stability

format_1_state_log.event_description: Atlantis_Data_Lag: POA phone number transposed in the system queue.

format_2_triples.situation: Daughter threatens to refuse discharge due to fear of patient falling.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Walked daughter through Day 1 expectations and the role of the MA visit.

format_2_triples.situation: SW needs to know if the patient's home is ready for the transition.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Conducted 24-hour pre-discharge pulse call to verify home readiness.

format_2_triples.situation: Daughter requests medical advice on managing patient's chemo-induced nausea.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Escalated clinical concerns to the Social Worker and floor nurse.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Step back and allow the Social Worker to manage the daughter's anxiety and the potential discharge delay without PN interference.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Respects the SW boundary regarding discharge timing and family counseling (Stage 2: Maintenance).

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Provide a physical appointment card to the daughter and confirm the 24-hour MA visit schedule to provide a concrete safety net.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Follows the Scheduling Anchor and Patient Education protocols (Stage 3: Handoff).

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Advise the daughter on which HHA agency has the best oncology nurses and help her fill out the insurance authorization forms to speed up the process.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Violates the Banned Actions list by suggesting specific agencies and handling insurance/clinical-related paperwork.
